result:
I’d make it much harder for people to forget that other people are real.

Not just “be nicer,” but a deep, instinctive awareness that the person across the border, across the argument, across the screen, across the factory floor, across the prison wall, has an inner life as vivid and urgent as one’s own.

A lot of the world’s worst things depend on abstraction: turning people into numbers, enemies, markets, demographics, collateral, “them.” If that reflex weakened, I think many other changes would follow — less cruelty, less indifference, less appetite for humiliation, fewer wars sold as inevitabilities, fewer systems designed around hidden suffering.

Not perfect harmony. People would still disagree, compete, fail, and hurt each other.

But it would be harder to build a world on denial.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
